name: Wing Aspect Ratio Calculator
description: Calculate the aspect ratio of an aircraft wing, a key factor in aerodynamic efficiency.
formulaDescription: Aspect Ratio (AR) is the square of the wingspan (b) divided by the wing area (S).

example.explanation: With a wingspan of 12m and an area of 18m², the aspect ratio is (12 * 12) / 18 = 144 / 18 = 8.

faqs.question: What is a high aspect ratio?
faqs.answer: Gliders typically have high aspect ratios (20-30), which reduces induced drag and improves glide efficiency.

faqs.question: Why do fighter jets have low aspect ratios?
faqs.answer: Low aspect ratio wings (like delta wings) are stronger and perform better at supersonic speeds, though they are less efficient at low speeds.

concept: Aspect ratio is a measure of how long and slender a wing is compared to its width (chord).
guideSteps: Enter the total wingspan of the aircraft.
Enter the total surface area of the wing.
The calculator will compute the ratio of span to chord.
